title: Austin Unsupervised Fleet Grows to 13 Active Vehicles
summary: Tesla's Austin fleet shows 13 unsupervised Model Y vehicles active in the last 30 days, representing the highest confirmed driverless count for the core scaling market. This reflects gradual but measurable progression in the primary deployment zone.
impact_reason: Modest positive progress in Austin's unsupervised fleet—slow but steady growth that validates operational feasibility, though absolute scale remains far below stated targets.

title: Tesla Unsupervised Robotaxi Fleet Reaches 10 Vehicles in Austin
summary: Tesla's Austin robotaxi fleet hit 10 unsupervised Model Y vehicles, marking the highest confirmed count for driverless service in the city. This reflects ongoing ramp-up in the primary operational hub.
impact_reason: Confirmed growth to 10 unsupervised vehicles in Austin signals clear progress in fleet size expansion, a key gating factor for scaling velocity in the core Texas market.

title: Tesla Expands Robotaxi to Dallas and Houston, Tripling Texas Footprint
summary: Tesla launched unsupervised robotaxi service in Dallas and Houston on April 18, marking expansion beyond Austin with geofenced zones covering approximately 25 square miles per city. The move establishes three active Texas hubs as part of a planned H1 2026 seven-city rollout.
impact_reason: Meaningful positive signal for scaling momentum—adds two new markets with unsupervised operations simultaneously, though initial fleet deployment remains minimal (1-2 vehicles per city), indicating Austin remains the primary scaling hub.

title: Tesla Austin Robotaxis Involved in 14 Crashes Since Launch
summary: A February regulatory filing revealed Tesla's Austin robotaxis have been involved in 14 crashes since the service launched, indicating safety incidents during the ramping phase.
impact_reason: Mildly negative for scaling velocity—safety incidents create regulatory scrutiny risk and could trigger operational constraints, though incidents alone have not yet paused Austin expansion.
